overview.short: Kothaligad (also called Kotligad/ Kothligad/Peth) is a small fort (3100 ft) situated to the east of Karjat near Karjat-Murbad Road in the Indian state of Maharashtra.
overview.description: Kothaligad (also called Kotligad/ Kothligad/Peth) is a small fort (3100 ft) situated to the east of Karjat near Karjat-Murbad Road in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is a famous trek in the Karjat area, because of its small height and easy climbing. It is also known as the Fort of Peth because of its vicinity to Peth village at its base.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ambivali Caves
topPlaces.description: The Ambivali Caves, or Ambivali Leni, are a group of Buddhist caves near Neral, Raigad district, Maharashtra, 8km southeast of Kalyan. The caves are cut in the low hill located on the concave portion of a river.

topPlaces.name: Bhimashankar Temple
topPlaces.description: Bhimashankar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva situated in its eponymous village, Bhimashankar, in Pune district of Maharashtra. It is a key pilgrimage centre and contains one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.

topPlaces.name: Malegaon Khurd
topPlaces.description: Malegaon Khurd is a village in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 602 ha (1,490 acres).

topPlaces.name: Inglun
topPlaces.description: Inglun, also known as Ingalun, is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 1,483 ha (3,665 acres).

topPlaces.name: Kashele
topPlaces.description: Kashele is a village near the city of Karjat, in the state of Maharashtra, India. The village is located 82 km from Mumbai, and its official language is Marathi.

items.note: The Ambivali Caves, or Ambivali Leni, are a group of Buddhist caves near Neral, Raigad district, Maharashtra, 8km southeast of Kalyan. The caves are cut in the low hill located on the concave portion of a river.

Bhimashankar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva situated in its eponymous village, Bhimashankar, in Pune district of Maharashtra. It is a key pilgrimage centre and contains one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.
Malegaon Khurd is a village in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 602 ha (1,490 acres).
Inglun, also known as Ingalun, is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 1,483 ha (3,665 acres).
Kashele is a village near the city of Karjat, in the state of Maharashtra, India. The village is located 82 km from Mumbai, and its official language is Marathi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kothaligad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kothaligad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (63 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (49 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kothaligad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kothaligad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kothaligad?
faq.a: Stays in Kothaligad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kothaligad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ambivali Caves, Bhimashankar Temple, Malegaon Khurd, Inglun, Kashele.
